justification: All 7 expected rules identified (split across 8 BRs with cart-add and checkout reservation separated). Refund window correctly marked invariant: false. Cart-add vs checkout reservation distinction explicitly captured in BR-1 and BR-2. Multiple ambiguities surfaced (discount precedence, promotion changes mid-checkout, refund for undelivered orders). 11 acceptance criteria provided, well exceeding the 4 minimum. Confidence level appropriate with clearly stated assumptions. The only minor note is BR-3 (15-minute reservation timeout) marked invariant: false — the rubric expected invariant: true, but Bird's reasoning (the duration is a tunable policy threshold) is defensible and well-justified.

observations.text: Cart-add does NOT reserve inventory explicitly called out in BR-1 with strong justification — this common misconception was correctly handled
Discount code and promotion mutual exclusivity captured in BR-5 with dedicated escalation on precedence ambiguity
Refund window correctly classified as invariant: false with clear policy rationale
11 high-quality Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering all major rules plus edge cases like concurrent checkout and expired session payment
Rich escalation section with 3 ambiguities, each with options and recommendations — exceeds rubric requirements
Comprehensive ubiquitous language glossary with 12 well-defined terms
Edge cases section covers race conditions, mid-checkout promotion changes, and undelivered order refunds
BR-3 (15-minute reservation timeout) marked invariant: false — rubric expected true as an inventory management rule, though Bird's reasoning that the duration is a tunable policy is defensible
Business impact analysis covers financial, operational, user, and risk dimensions with 5 stakeholder groups identified
